decisions.decision: Split fresh `handle_event_trade(...)` handling into a side-effect-free active-market preflight and a later accepted-mutation phase: keep normalize plus idempotency replay/conflict first, preserve persisted terminal `409 market_not_active` behavior for inactive markets, and delay fresh accepted command/order creation until the active submission has passed the preview and max-position checks.
alternatives_rejected: Continue materializing the EventTrade command before the active-market or position-limit gates.
Convert inactive-market EventTrades to the new side-effect-free validation style instead of keeping their replayable terminal rejection contract.

decisions.decision: Keep `build_event_trade_position_net_change(...)` as the single netting primitive for both preflight preview and accepted exposure sync, and recompute the projection inside `sync_account_exposure_state(...)` from the current stored row instead of threading a richer preview object through the accepted path.
alternatives_rejected: Pass a richer precomputed preview object from `handle_event_trade(...)` into `sync_account_exposure_state(...)`.
Duplicate buy/sell netting arithmetic inline inside the sync helper.

decisions.decision: Refactor `sync_account_exposure_state(...)` around one computed `currentNetSize`/`signedDelta`/`resultingNetSize` projection, then branch cleanly into survivor updates versus exact-zero deletion so accepted buys, sells, and zero-crossing trades all share the same rounding and cleanup rules while surviving rows always advance their last-trade metadata.
alternatives_rejected: Call `ensure_account_exposure_position(...)` before computing the netting result and create placeholder rows even when the accepted trade immediately nets back to zero.
Refresh `lastTradePrice`, `updatedAt`, `lastOrderId`, and `lastCommandId` only when absolute exposure grows instead of on every non-zero survivor.

decisions.decision: Keep this implementation step confined to EventTrade accepted-path ordering and `ACCOUNT_EXPOSURE` mutation, leaving `ACCOUNT_RISK`, `sync_account_risk_state(...)`, `/v1/accounts/{id}/risk`, and the heavier focused regression additions for the later parent regression step.
alternatives_rejected: Broaden the patch into `/risk` or `ACCOUNT_RISK` changes.
Make this implementation step responsible for the full new regression suite instead of landing the mutation refactor first.

steps.task: Restructure the `handle_event_trade(...)` write-lock flow so normalization and idempotency replay/conflict handling remain first, previously persisted accepted or rejected EventTrades still replay immediately, and fresh inactive-market submissions continue through the existing replayable terminal `market_not_active` rejection path.

For fresh active submissions only, keep the preview/max-position gate as the last side-effect-free preflight and move `materialize_event_trade_command(...)` plus `create_event_trade_order(...)` behind that pass condition so no fresh accepted command or order exists before preflight succeeds.

Refactor `sync_account_exposure_state(...)` to read the current `(accountId, marketId, outcomeId)` row, reuse `build_event_trade_position_net_change(...)` once for the accepted order, and branch on the rounded `resultingNetSize` instead of coupling arithmetic to eager row creation.

Implement the accepted-path exposure mutations from that shared projection: create or update the surviving row when the rounded result is non-zero, always advance `netSize`, `lastTradePrice`, `updatedAt`, `lastOrderId`, and `lastCommandId` on survivors, and delete the row plus prune the account when the rounded result is exactly zero.

Run targeted EventTrade/account-exposure verification to confirm accepted buys, sells, zero-crossing cleanup, and metadata updates still work after the ordering change while `ACCOUNT_RISK` and `/v1/accounts/{id}/risk` remain untouched, leaving the broader dedicated regression additions to the later parent step.
